should_trigger: Convert these policy sentences into a controlled English form, first-order predicates, and a model-theoretic truth check with explicit object language and metalanguage.
Our product taxonomy has multiple inheritance and attribute combinations. Build a lattice-based ontology refinement plan and show which attributes define each concept.
Turn this library database rule set into relations, conceptual graph roles, quantifier scopes, and constraints that can be audited.
We have a knowledge graph with event nodes, state predicates, and n-ary relations. Decide whether to model it as tables, labeled graphs, hypergraphs, or bipartite conceptual graphs.
Check whether the sentence 'every employee has a manager' was formalized correctly, including the difference between forall-exists and exists-forall readings.
Design a controlled natural language for domain experts that maps function words and content words to logic without relying on ordinary English implicature.
Explain why our system should not use its own unrestricted truth predicate inside the same rule language, and propose a safe object-language/metalanguage split.
should_not_trigger: Summarize Sowa's biography in five bullet points.
Write a normal English rewrite of this paragraph with friendlier tone.
Implement a React graph visualization component without any semantic modeling requirements.
Calculate the union and intersection of two small sets for a homework exercise.
Give a high-level opinion about whether correspondence theory of truth is philosophically correct.
edge_cases: The user asks for a database schema but the rules include quantifiers, negation, and n-ary events; apply only the representation-selection and relation-arity parts before recommending a schema.
The user only needs a glossary, but terms include subtype, equivalence class, and transitive closure; trigger a lightweight pass to prevent inconsistent definitions.
The user has natural-language requirements with words like 'and' and 'the'; trigger only if those words affect parsing, reference resolution, or logical commitments.
